perClaim.claim: Focus on steady energy release with complex carbs and lean proteins
Include iodine-rich foods to support thyroid function
Ensure adequate iron and selenium intake
Prioritize whole foods over processed options
Stay hydrated with water and herbal teas
Grilled chicken salad with quinoa and olive oil dressing
Baked salmon with sweet potato and steamed broccoli
Scrambled eggs with spinach and whole grain toast
Turkey and avocado wrap with mixed greens
Lean beef stir-fry with brown rice and vegetables
Smoothie with banana, spinach, protein powder, and almond milk
Lentil soup with whole grain bread
Grilled tofu with quinoa and roasted vegetables
Whole grain pancakes with berries and maple syrup
Chickpea and vegetable salad with feta cheese
Baked cod with roasted potatoes and green beans
Orange and a handful of almonds
Yogurt parfait with granola and mixed berries
Tuna salad sandwich on whole grain bread
Chicken and vegetable skewers with couscous
Black bean burger on whole grain bun with side salad
Pork tenderloin with mashed sweet potatoes and asparagus
Pear and a handful of walnuts
French toast with berries and Greek yogurt
Quinoa bowl with roasted vegetables and tahini dressing
Beef and vegetable stew with whole grain roll
Consume meals at regular intervals to maintain steady energy levels
Include iodine-rich foods like seafood, dairy, and eggs to support thyroid function
Opt for complex carbohydrates like oats, quinoa, and sweet potatoes for sustained energy
Stay hydrated with at least 2 liters of water daily
Consider consulting with your healthcare provider regarding hypothyroidism management
